scope: FQ-08: block and enemy damage visuals — make destruction readable before it completes.
summary: Blocks: player.mine_damage_stage() maps mining progress to stages 0-3 and drives a crack overlay drawn on the target cell while the mine action is held — deterministic per cell (member RNG re-seeded from hash(mine_target) each frame; no flicker, no per-frame allocation), stage x 3 short lines clamped inside the tile, layered over the existing highlight and progress bar. Transience is guaranteed three ways: the overlay never enters cells/deltas/saves (world.gd untouched, zero save growth per the queue's explicit constraint); the existing _reset_mining clears it on target change, reach/can_mine failure, and release; and save_manager.apply_state now resets in-memory mining so a load can never resurrect partial damage. Enemies: a mini health bar (health_bar_ratio fill) appears above any enemy with hp < max_hp on both the FQ-07 art path and the drawn-rect fallback, keeping the existing tint/lighten damage cues — damage is visible well before death. Drops, mining frame counts (dirt 21 / wood 33 / stone 66 baselines), and save/load semantics are untouched.
acceptance: A stone block visibly changes before breaking: stage 0 -> 2 at 60% of required mining time (fq08_block_damage_stages)
Enemy damage is visible before death: hurt-bar ratio 1.00 -> 0.67 after a non-lethal hit with the enemy alive (fq08_enemy_hurt_visible)
Visuals do not alter drop counts: finishing the mine yields exactly one stone (fq08_drops_unchanged); a non-lethal hit rolls zero drops
Visuals do not alter save/load: 50% partial damage + save + load leaves the block intact at stage 0 (fq08_damage_never_saved); block damage is transient by design
Visual damage resets on target change and on mining stop (fq08_stage_resets), plus on load
Smoke 179/179 (was 173) on windowed Godot 4.6.1; validator PASS; capsule doctor healthy; git diff --check clean
validation: COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 waited windowed run -> 179/179 PASS incl. six fq08_* checks
decisions: Block damage lives entirely in the player's transient mining state and is drawn by the player overlay — no per-block damage storage, honoring the queue's no-save-growth constraint literally (zero bytes added)
Cracks are deterministic per cell (seeded from the target) so they read as damage rather than noise; endpoints clamp to the tile
apply_state clears in-memory mining on load (review-adjacent hardening): a restored world must not inherit the pre-load mining target
Enemy feedback is a minimal hurt bar over both draw paths rather than posture/animation stages — those belong to a future art-driven pass on the FQ-07 pipeline
Recon agent skipped (all surfaces already in-session); sonnet review ran: no must-fix or should-fix, three nits applied (RNG reuse, crack clamping, comment clarity)
risks: Crack and bar aesthetics are programmer art; FQ-07's pipeline can supply real per-stage crack sprites later
Hurt bar width is fixed at 16px — revisit if oversized enemy art arrives
Stage thresholds (25/50/75%) untested by human play
next_action: FQ-09: visual inventory, toolbelt, and village panels.
